message:
feat(fsfs): tighten root probe limits, expand excluded dirs, and improve first-run UX

Root probe tuning:
- Reduce ROOT_PROBE_MAX_DEPTH from 3 to 2 to avoid scanning excessively
  deep directory trees during automatic index-root discovery. Depth 3 was
  causing long waits on home directories with deeply nested structures.
- Lower ROOT_PROBE_MAX_ENTRIES_PER_DIR from 2048 to 512 to skip over
  large flat directories (e.g. node_modules siblings) more quickly.
- Reduce ROOT_PROBE_MAX_TOTAL_ENTRIES from 50,000 to 10,000 so the
  probe aborts earlier on extremely large filesystems, keeping startup
  latency predictable.

Expanded directory exclusion list for is_probe_excluded_dir_name():
- macOS system/media directories: Library, Pictures, Movies, Music,
  Photos Library.photoslibrary, .Trash, Applications, .Spotlight-V100,
  .fseventsd, .DocumentRevisions-V100. These never contain indexable
  source code and scanning them wastes time and I/O.
- Windows system directories: AppData, Windows, Program Files,
  Program Files (x86).
- Common large non-code tool directories: .local, .rustup, .bun,
  .nvm, .pyenv. These contain binaries and caches, not user code.

First-run UX improvement:
- Print "Scanning for indexable directories..." to stderr before the
  probe runs, and print the candidate count when finished. Previously
  the CLI appeared frozen during probe with no feedback.

Model materialization logging:
- Replace the warn-only error log with structured info-level logging
  that reports models_written and bytes_written when bundled semantic
  models are freshly materialized. This makes it visible in logs when
  models are extracted for the first time vs. already present.

Formatting:
- Apply rustfmt to long lines, closure indentation, struct literal
  formatting in tests, and match arm wrapping throughout runtime.rs,
  watcher.rs, and e2e_recall.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
